analysis.consistency: The data entry is consistent with standard D&D 5e feature documentation structure, identifying the feature name, class association, level of acquisition, and a descriptive text.
analysis.quality: The quality is high. The `slug`, `name`, `class`, and `level` fields are precise. The `description` accurately reflects the core mechanic (choosing a fighting style at 2nd level) and the associated restriction (cannot choose the same style twice).
analysis.mechanics: The feature grants the Ranger class access to a Fighting Style choice at level 2. This is a standard class progression mechanic in D&D 5e. The mechanic is clearly explained: choose one option from a defined list (not provided here, but implied by 'Choose one of the following options').

recommendations: Ensure the associated list of selectable Fighting Styles (the options referenced in the description) is linked or fully documented elsewhere for complete mechanical context.
